title: Config write, field-by-field repair, and allowed_roots breadth guard
action: Add a src/config/write.rs layer of pure, unit-testable functions with no prompting and no terminal use. Provide: (a) a validated persist that re-runs Config::load_str on the candidate JSON and refuses to write when invalid, per D6; (b) field-by-field repair per D7 that takes the existing raw file plus a map of replacement values, preserves every currently-valid field, and returns which fields are invalid so a caller can ask about only those; (c) a backup-then-recreate fallback per D7 for the case where the file cannot be parsed as JSON at all, writing config.json.bak-<timestamp> BEFORE any overwrite; (d) a breadth classifier per D9 that reports whether a candidate allowed_roots path is over-broad (filesystem root, the user's home directory, or a symlink) so a caller can demand typed confirmation. Nothing in doctor calls this yet.

truths: An invalid candidate config is never persisted; the existing file is left untouched.
Field-by-field repair preserves every field that was already valid and reports exactly which fields need a new value.
The backup file is written and verified to exist before the original is overwritten, and only on the unparseable path.
The breadth classifier flags filesystem root, the home directory, and symlinked paths as over-broad, and does not flag an ordinary narrow directory.
Writes are atomic enough that an interrupted write cannot leave a truncated config.json in place of a valid one.

artifacts.substantive: validated persist, field-by-field repair, timestamped backup fallback, and allowed_roots breadth classifier, each unit-tested

key_links: src/config/write.rs reuses Config::load_str for the D6 pre-persist validation instead of reimplementing validation
prohibitions: Do not prompt, read stdin, or touch the terminal in this cell.
Do not weaken or bypass any existing config validation, including the empty-allowed_roots fail-closed rule.
Do not allow any secret or token field to be written into config.json.
Do not overwrite a user's config.json on any path that has not first written a verified backup.

trace.outcome: Added src/config/write.rs: validated persist (reuses Config::load_str, D6), field-by-field repair with per-field diagnosis dropping unknown/secret keys (D7), timestamped-backup-then-recreate fallback for unparseable files (D7), and an allowed_roots breadth classifier flagging filesystem root, home directory, and symlinks (D9). Atomic write via temp-file-then-rename. mod.rs gained one additive 'pub mod write;' line. Nothing calls this yet, matching the cell scope.
trace.verify_output: cargo test: 124 passed (lib), 2 passed, 3 passed, 0 passed (doc tests) — all 0 failed. cargo clippy -D warnings: silent (no warnings). Exit 0.
